evals.prompt:
Edit this commit message to be clearer:

'This commit delves into the authentication module and makes some pivotal changes that leverage the new JWT library to foster a more robust and seamless login experience for our users, ensuring that the enduring legacy of security is maintained.'
evals.expected_output: Rewrites the commit message to be concise, active, and free of AI-style puffery
expectations: Removes puffery words (pivotal, leverage, foster, robust, seamless, enduring legacy, delves)
Result is significantly shorter than the original
Uses concrete language describing what actually changed
Final message follows conventional commit style or is otherwise concise

evals.prompt: Write an error message for when a user tries to upload a file larger than 10MB.
evals.expected_output: A clear, specific, positive-form error message that tells the user what to do
expectations: Uses positive form (tells user what to do, not just what went wrong)
Is concise (one or two sentences max)
Uses concrete language (no vague terms like 'issue encountered')
Does not use puffery or promotional language

evals.prompt:
Rewrite this documentation paragraph:

'This groundbreaking feature represents a cutting-edge approach to data processing that seamlessly integrates with your existing workflow. By leveraging our robust pipeline architecture, it fosters enhanced throughput — enabling users to process multifaceted datasets with unprecedented efficiency. The feature delves deep into the realm of parallel computing to deliver transformative results.'
evals.expected_output: Rewrites the paragraph with concrete language, active voice, and no AI puffery words
expectations: Removes all AI puffery (groundbreaking, cutting-edge, seamlessly, robust, fosters, multifaceted, unprecedented, delves, realm, transformative)
Replaces vague claims with specific, concrete descriptions
Result is shorter than the original
Explains what the feature actually does in plain language
